2605.05478.title: LANTERN: LLM-Augmented Neurosymbolic Transfer with Experience-Gated Reasoning Networks
2605.05478.paper_summary: Transfer learning in reinforcement learning (RL) seeks to accelerate learning in new tasks by leveraging knowledge from related sources. Existing neurosymbolic transfer methods, however, typically rely on manually specified task automata, assume a single source task, and use fixed knowledge-integration mechanisms that cannot adapt to varying source relevance. We propose LANTERN, a unified framework for multi-source neurosymbolic transfer that addresses these limitations through three components: (i) deterministic finite automata generated from natural language task descriptions using large language models, (ii) semantic embedding-based aggregation of multiple source policies weighted by cross-task similarity, and (iii) adaptive teacher-student gating based on temporal-difference error and semantic uncertainty. Across domains spanning resource management, navigation, and control, LANTERN achieves 40-60% improvements in sample efficiency over existing baselines while remaining robust to poorly aligned sources. These results demonstrate that multi-source, adaptively weighted neurosymbolic transfer can improve scalability and robustness in symbolic RL settings.
2605.05478.paper_summary_zh: 轉移學習在強化學習 (RL) 中旨在通過利用相關來源的知識來加速新任務的學習。 然而，現有的神經符號轉移方法通常依賴於手動指定的任務自動機，假設單一來源任務，並使用固定的知識整合機制，無法適應不同來源的重要性。 我們提出了 LANTERN，一個統一的多來源神經符號轉移框架，通過三個組件來解決這些限制：(i) 使用大型語言模型從自然語言任務描述生成的確定性有限自動機，(ii) 基於語義嵌入的多來源策略聚合，根據跨任務相似性加權，以及 (iii) 基於時間差錯和語義不確定性的自適應教師-學生閘控。 在資源管理、導航和控制等多個領域中， LANTERN 在樣本效率上比現有基準提高了 40-60%，同時對於不良對齊的來源保持穩健。 這些結果表明，多來源、自適應加權的神經符號轉移可以提高符號 RL 環境中的可擴展性和穩健性。
2605.05478.author: Mahyar Alinejad et.al.
2605.05478.authors: Mahyar Alinejad, Yue Wang, Amrit Singh Bedi, George Atia
